goal: Hub default output dir: <os-temp>/mainline-hub/<repo-basename> instead of .mainline/hub; export's dir arg now optional

summary.title: Hub: default output to <os-temp>/mainline-hub/<repo>, dir arg optional
summary.what: Move the default `mainline hub` output directory from `.mainline/hub` (inside the repo's config dir) to `<os.TempDir()>/mainline-hub/<repo-basename>`. New helper `defaultHubDir(repoRoot)` is the single source of truth, called from both `hub open` and `hub export`. `hub export`'s positional arg becomes optional (MaximumNArgs(1)) — running it with no args now writes to the same default `hub open` uses. Agent guidance template bumped to v11 to document the new default.
summary.why: Reader feedback: the prior default put the static site under `.mainline/`, the same directory that holds canonical config (`.mainline/config.toml`). That polluted the config dir, required adding more entries to `.gitignore`, and conflated 'place where Mainline writes durable state' with 'place where Mainline writes throwaway exports'. The static site is throwaway by definition — it should live in OS-managed scratch space, not in the repo.
summary.user_goal: Run `mainline hub open` (or `mainline hub export` with no args) and have the site land somewhere predictable, never inside git, with no configuration ceremony.

decisions.rationale: User said '/tmp/.mainline 怎么样'; honoured the intent (move out of repo, predictable path) while making it portable (Windows has no /tmp; macOS resolves /tmp via /private/tmp anyway) and per-repo (basename namespace stops two repos clobbering each other's exports).

decisions.chose: Cobra MaximumNArgs(1); falls back to defaultHubDir when omitted.
decisions.rationale: Two different paths to the same default would be a maintenance trap. One helper, two call sites.

decisions.chose: v10 → v11; document the default in the same migration cycle.
decisions.rationale: Without the template update, the agent-guidance doc would still say `.mainline/hub` and reach a future agent that pulls the new code with a stale instruction. Versions are cheap; staleness is expensive.

rejected.reason: Adding scratch paths to gitignore is exactly the workaround we're removing. The right fix is to put scratch where scratch belongs.

rejected.alternative: Use literal `/tmp/.mainline` like the user wrote.
rejected.reason: Cross-platform breakage (Windows) and per-repo collisions. The user's intent was 'move it out of the repo' — `os.TempDir()` namespaced by basename does that without locking us to one OS or one repo.

risks: Users who scripted `mainline hub export ./hub` and depended on the explicit positional behaviour are unaffected; users who scripted `cat .mainline/hub/data/intents.json` will have to update the path. No existing PR / doc references that pattern outside of agents-md, which is updated in the same PR.

anti_patterns.what: Default scratch output inside the repo's config or working tree
anti_patterns.why: Conflates durable state with throwaway state, forces .gitignore maintenance, and surprises users who run a 'view' command and find new untracked files.

followups: Consider adding `mainline hub clean` to delete the default directory if a user wants to reclaim disk.
If multiple agents on the same machine work on the same repo, they share the default dir — fine for now but worth flagging if it ever causes a rebuild race.

architectural_claims: Throwaway static-export output belongs in OS-managed scratch space, not under the repo's config dir.
Both hub commands resolve the default through a single helper so the rule lives in one place.
behavioral_changes: `mainline hub open` and `mainline hub export` (no args) now write to <os-temp>/mainline-hub/<repo-basename>.
`mainline hub export` accepts 0 or 1 positional args (was: required).
Agent guidance template at v11; existing repos see update_available.

api_changes.signature: mainline hub open default path moved from .mainline/hub to <os-temp>/mainline-hub/<repo>

data_model_changes.name: agents-md template version
data_model_changes.migration_notes: Bumped to v11. mainline agents update migrates in place; user content outside markers preserved.

security_implications: OS temp dirs are typically world-readable on POSIX. The exported HTML is the same data the agent already has access to via mainline log/show — no new disclosure surface — but operators on shared machines should be aware.
migration_notes: Run `mainline agents update` to pick up v11.
Scripts that read `.mainline/hub/...` files must switch to the new default or pass an explicit dir to `mainline hub export <dir>`.
